findings.evidence: Nearby `Contractable.measurePreserving_reindex` is already arbitrary in `φ` (`lines 134-136`), and the new proof’s internal `hreindex` is also `∀ φ : ℕ → ℕ, StrictMono φ → ...` (`lines 170-176`); only the exported statement and `side` specialize this to `r + n` (`lines 162-180`).
findings.fix: Prove a general lemma with conclusion `μ.map (fun ω => (X j ω, fun n => X (φ n) ω)) = μ.map (fun ω => (X k ω, fun n => X (φ n) ω))`, hypotheses `(hφ : StrictMono φ) (hj : j < φ 0) (hk : k < φ 0)`, and derive the current cutoff-tail version by taking `φ n := r + n`.
findings.issue: `Contractable.pairLaw_eq` exposes only the special tail `fun n => r + n`; the natural contractability-level statement is for any `φ : ℕ → ℕ` with `StrictMono φ` and `j,k < φ 0`.

summary: The exported pair-law lemma is under-generalized relative to the surrounding contractability API: it fixes the common tail to the consecutive sequence `r+n` even though the proof and nearby theorem already work for any strictly increasing common future subsequence.
